# terrible_community_mongodb_mongodb_atlas_cluster (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the cluster as it appears in Atlas. Once the cluster is created, its name cannot be changed.
- `provider_settings` (String) Configuration for the provisioned servers on which MongoDB runs. The available options are specific to the cloud service provider.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auto_scaling` (String) Configure your cluster to automatically scale its storage and cluster tier.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cluster_type` (String) Type of the cluster that you want to create.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `disk_size_gb` (Number) Capacity, in gigabytes, of the host's root volume. Increase this number to add capacity, up to a maximum possible value of 4096 (i.e., 4 TB). This value must be a positive integer.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `mongo_db_major_version` (String) Version of the cluster to deploy. Atlas always deploys the cluster with the latest stable release of the specified version. You can upgrade to a newer version of MongoDB when you modify a cluster.
- `pit_enabled` (Boolean) Flag that indicates the cluster uses continuous cloud backups.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `provider_backup_enabled` (Boolean) Flag that indicates if the cluster uses Cloud Backups for backups.
- `replication_factor` (Number) Number of replica set members. Each member keeps a copy of your databases, providing high availability and data redundancy.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
